With powerful AI Agents embedded across our platform, teams can analyze, test, and optimize user experiences faster than ever. Ranked #1 across multiple categories in G2’s Winter 2026 Report, Amplitude is the best-in-class solution for product, data, and marketing teams. Learn more at amplitude.com . As an organization, we deliver for our customers by living our values. We operate from a place of humility, take ownership of problems and successes, approach challenges with a growth mindset, and put our customers at the center of everything we do. Amplitude’s Commitment to Diversity Equity & Inclusion (DEI): Amplitude believes that diversity enables the creation of better products, improves the ability to solve complex problems, and drives more powerful solutions. We strive to create an environment of inclusion—one focused on psychological safety, empathy, and human connection—that will allow employees of all backgrounds to thrive. About The Role & Team Amplitude is building analytics for the agent era. Our Agents can investigate, create signals and surface patterns nobody thought to look for. As our Principal Product Marketing Manager for Agents, you’ll define how we tell that story to the market—from our agent capabilities themselves to the observability layer that helps teams understand if their agents are actually driving results. This is a category-defining role. You’ll shape how developers, product teams, and enterprise buyers think about agents, observability, and evaluation. You’ll partner closely with Product, Engineering, and GTM teams to turn complex systems into narratives that actually land—and evolve them as fast as the market does. If you’d rather define a category than inherit one, you’ll feel right at home. As a Principal Product Marketing Manager, you will: Define and own Amplitude’s point of view on agent analytics —what “good” looks like, how teams should evaluate agents, and why behavioral data is critical to improving real-world outcomes (not just model benchmarks) Turn deeply technical concepts into market-shaping narratives —connecting agent architecture (LLM evals, context engineering, tracing, orchestration) to business impact Lead 0→1 launches for agent capabilities —building positioning, demos, and technical deep-dives that reflect how teams actually build today using tools like Cursor, Claude Code, LangChain, and internal eval frameworks Create proof that moves skeptical, technical buyers — Work with customers to establish best practices and learnings that drive adoption. Turn customer feedback from AI builders, startups, and enterprise teams into stronger signals for the product roadmap. Build the case studies and best practices that drive adoption. Build and iterate GTM motion for the builder audienc e—reaching AI-native builders where they are (developer communities, Discord, X, hackathons), testing hands-on experiences over static content, and refining based on what actually drives adoption Act as the connective tissue between Product, Engineering, and GTM —bringing back insights from teams building with tools like OpenAI, Anthropic, and custom agent stacks to influence roadmap and positioning You'll be a great addition to the team if you have: Experience working with AI-native products, developer tools, and a point of view on where this space is going Strong technical curiosity (and ideally fluency) in areas like LLM evals, agent architecture, and context management A knack for multi-audience storytelling—you know when to speak like an engineer, a PM, or an executive (and when not to mix the three) A bias toward building over talking—you’d rather ship a demo, test it with real users, and iterate than debate messaging in a doc Comfort operating in ambiguity—you don’t wait for a playbook, you start writing it At a minimum, you need to have: 6–7+ years of product marketing experience in developer tools, AI/ML, or highly technical products Experience leading product launches and driving cross-functional alignment across Product, Engineering, Sales, and Customer Success Demonstrated ability to translate technical concepts into clear positioning and messaging that resonates with target audiences Experience working with or marketing to technical buyers such as developers, data teams, or AI product teams Strong communication skills and the ability to adapt messaging based on audience, context, and market maturity Our values: At Amplitude, our values guide how we show up for one another and for our customers: Humility: We operate from a place of empathy and openness, seeking to understand many points of view. Ownership: We take the initiative to solve problems that drive our shared company success. Growth Mindset: We’re tenacious in the face of challenges and seek feedback in order to grow ourselves and others. Customer Centricity: We put the customer at the center of everything we do and are deeply committed to their success. About Mixpanel Mixpanel turns data clarity into innovation. Trusted by more than 29,000 companies, including Workday, Pinterest, LG, and Rakuten Viber, Mixpanel’s AI-first digital analytics help teams accelerate adoption, improve retention, and ship with confidence. Powering this is an industry-leading platform that combines product and web analytics, session replay, experimentation, feature flags, and metric trees. Mixpanel delivers insights that customers trust. Visit mixpanel.com to learn more. About the Team Mixpanel’s Product organization is built on small, autonomous pods — engineering, product, and design working side by side with direct access to customer data, feedback, and business metrics. We operate from first principles, challenge assumptions, and bias toward simple, elegant solutions. Product owns execution end to end. The Product Platform team’s mission is to make Mixpanel easy to manage, secure, compliant, and built on a reliable foundation. The team runs two engineering tracks: a server infrastructure and application security track, and an enterprise management and UI track. Together they own the systems that every Mixpanel customer — and every internal product team — depends on. About the Role As Mixpanel scales into the enterprise, the platform that underpins how customers manage, secure, and control their Mixpanel environment becomes increasingly strategic. This isn’t a role about a single feature or workflow — it’s about the foundational systems that determine whether a 500-person company trusts us with their data and their organizational complexity. The Senior PM, Platform will own Mixpanel’s core enterprise management surface: RBAC and permissions, SSO and passkeys, authentication services, bulk user and workspace management, audit logs, admin controls, and settings UX. You’ll be the PM for the systems that enterprise IT and security teams evaluate when deciding whether to standardize on Mixpanel. This role requires someone who thinks in systems, understands the security and compliance requirements of enterprise customers, and knows how to build trust — with engineering, with customers, and with the cross-functional teams that depend on platform stability. If you’re energized by infrastructure that quietly powers everything and occasionally takes center stage during a security review, this role is for you. description: Replit is the agentic software creation platform that enables anyone to build applications using natural language. With millions of users worldwide, Replit is democratizing software development by removing traditional barriers to application creation. About the Role We're looking for an AI-native product growth lead to own Replit's entire paid acquisition and lifecycle marketing engine as the single DRI. This is an IC role for someone who builds systems, not just campaigns. Someone who uses AI and automation aggressively to do what traditionally requires an entire team. You don't need an engineering background. You need the instinct to build: when you see a repetitive task, you reach for Replit or an API before you reach for a spreadsheet. You'll have data science and data engineering partners for infrastructure and modeling, and brand marketing will set creative direction. Your job is to turn that direction into a closed-loop optimization system: a self-improving system that turns performance data into better creative, optimizes spend across channels in near-real-time, and compounds every insight so nothing learned is ever lost. Each cycle, the system gets smarter. That's the engine you'll build and own. The ideal candidate has deep channel expertise across paid search, paid social, and lifecycle, but their real edge is building AI-powered workflows that scale creative production, automate measurement, and compound institutional knowledge. You Will Own full-funnel performance marketing across paid search & social, ASO, and lifecycle Build a self-improving creative engine: AI-driven ad generation, testing, and iteration that scales without scaling headcount Maintain a persistent knowledge layer so every experiment, result, and creative insight compounds automatically into the next cycle Own conversion signal quality end-to-end: right events, right audiences, right attribution, partnering with DE on the infrastructure Run a structured experimentation program where every test feeds a feedback loop, not a one-off report Automate performance reporting and alerting — your system flags problems (creative fatigue, CAC shifts, payback drift) before anyone has to ask Build geo-differentiated strategies across market tiers and decompose performance by region before diagnosing aggregate shifts Build the system that allocates spend, prioritizes tests, and rotates under-performers — the math makes the decisions; you build and own the engine that produces them How You'll Work This is not a traditional marketing role. You'll operate more like a growth EM or PM who happens to focus on paid channels. AI-First Execution: You automate before you do anything manually. Your primary tools are code, APIs, and AI workflows — not spreadsheets and platform UIs. Systems Over Tasks: You build loops, not checklists. Every manual process is a candidate for automation; every insight feeds back into the system. Strategic Partnership: You orchestrate brand, data science, and data engineering into a cohesive performance engine. Marketing feeds the system with creative direction and assets; you build the optimization loop that tests, scales winners, and returns performance insights to sharpen the next creative cycle. Bias to Action: You ship fast, learn fast, and design experiments that generate the data you need rather than waiting for perfect information. Required Skills and Experience 5+ years in performance marketing across at least two major paid channels (search, social, ASO, or programmatic) Hands-on experience building AI-powered marketing workflows — not just prompting, but durable systems that run and improve on their own Deep understanding of conversion tracking, attribution, and signal quality — you know the difference between filtering conversions and excluding audiences Data-literate: comfortable with SQL, dashboards, and statistical rigor without needing a data team to interpret results Track record managing significant budgets with clear ROI accountability Strong experimentation instincts — structured test-and-learn programs, not one-off A/B tests Preferred Qualifications You've built your own tooling for marketing operations — bonus if you can show a portfolio of what you've built Experience in developer-focused or PLG companies where paid and organic reinforce each other You've thrived as a solo operator or on a very lean team Comfortable with a modern data stack and warehouse-driven workflows Hands-on experience with server-side conversion APIs and the postback lifecycle You've brought measurement rigor and experimentation culture to a team that didn't have it before You've scaled paid acquisition across geos with dramatically different unit economics What You Won't Be Doing Managing a team—this is an IC role with leverage through systems, not headcount Defining messaging, narrative, or creative strategy—Marketing owns what we say and how it looks; you own where it goes, how it performs, and what we learn from it Building data pipelines from scratch—your DE partner handles infrastructure; you define requirements and consume the outputs Sitting in meetings all day—we want someone who ships, not someone who presents This is a full-time role that can be held from our Foster City, CA office. The role has an in-office requirement of Monday, Wednesday, and Friday. description: Replit is the agentic software creation platform that enables anyone to build applications using natural language. With millions of users worldwide, Replit is democratizing software development by removing traditional barriers to application creation. Replit sits at the center of one of the most consequential shifts in technology: AI is rewriting how software gets built and who builds it. Our platform enables anyone — entrepreneurs, operators, designers, students, and yes, engineers too — to go from idea to production-grade application through natural language. Our audience spans individual creators building their first app, startups shipping products at unprecedented speed, and enterprises deploying agentic software creation at scale. Telling that story — clearly, credibly, and in a way that moves markets — is the job. We're looking for a VP of Product Marketing to build and lead the PMM function at Replit. You'll own positioning, messaging, competitive intelligence, launches, and sales enablement across a dual go-to-market motion that includes both product-led growth and enterprise sales. You'll translate deep technical capabilities — agentic code generation, multi-model orchestration, cloud-native deployment — into narratives that resonate with builders of all backgrounds and the business leaders investing in this new paradigm. This is a building role. You'll architect the PMM operating model, hire a high-performing team, and establish the frameworks that connect product innovation to market impact. You'll work shoulder-to-shoulder with Product and Sales to ensure that every launch, every piece of content, and every competitive conversation reflects a clear, differentiated point of view on where software creation is going — and why Replit is leading it. This is a leadership role reporting directly to the CMO. What You'll Do Own Replit's product positioning and messaging. You'll define how the market understands Replit — not just what it does, but why it matters. You'll develop messaging frameworks that work across a complex audience landscape: technical users who care about power and flexibility, non-technical builders who care about accessibility and speed, and enterprise buyers who care about security, scale, and ROI. You'll pressure-test positioning continuously and evolve it as the market moves. Drive product launches that create market impact. You'll own the launch process end-to-end — from early roadmap integration with Product and Engineering through to the go-to-market execution that drives awareness, adoption, and revenue. Every launch should have a clear narrative, a measurable objective, and a plan that drives both organic growth and enterprise pipeline. Build competitive intelligence into a strategic advantage. The AI software creation landscape is crowded and moving fast. You'll build and maintain a structured competitive intelligence program — battlecards, win/loss analysis, market landscape maps — that gives Sales real weapons in the field and gives leadership a clear-eyed view of where Replit wins and where it needs to sharpen its edge. Enable Sales with content and tools they actually use. You'll create sales enablement materials — pitch decks, one-pagers, objection handling guides, ROI frameworks — that accelerate deal velocity and improve win rates. You'll build tight feedback loops with Sales so enablement stays current and grounded in what buyers actually care about. Support both PLG and SLG go-to-market motions. You'll partner with Consumer and Growth Marketing to ensure product messaging drives user acquisition, activation, and conversion — fueling Replit's product-led growth engine. You'll work across demand generation and field marketing teams to build content, campaigns, and narratives that generate and accelerate the enterprise pipeline. You'll understand how these motions differ, how they reinforce each other, and how PMM creates leverage across both. Translate technical depth into plain-speak storytelling. Replit's platform is deeply capable. You'll work closely with Engineering and Product to understand what's being built, why it matters, and how to express it in language that a non-technical founder, a CIO, and a platform engineer all find compelling — without dumbing it down for any of them. Set a high bar for content quality. You'll ensure that everything PMM produces — from a launch blog post to an analyst briefing to a competitive one-pager — reflects Replit's voice: clear, confident, and free of jargon. You'll care about craft, and you'll build a team that does too. Build the foundation for Partner Marketing. As Replit's partner ecosystem grows, you'll own the marketing narratives that support technology partnerships, platform integrations, and co-marketing motions. You'll develop positioning frameworks that articulate joint value propositions and create scalable co-marketing playbooks — laying the groundwork for a function that will scale alongside the business. Build and lead a high-performing PMM team. You'll recruit and develop product marketers who combine strategic thinking, technical curiosity, and executional speed. You'll install operating rhythms, define roles clearly, and create a culture where great work ships on time. Product Manager, Orchestration (Flows) About the Team Klaviyo’s Core Product team is responsible for the foundational experiences that power how marketers build, automate, and manage customer engagement. This includes the core surfaces and systems behind campaigns, flows, forms, profiles, lists, and segments — the building blocks brands rely on to create personalized, automated marketing programs. A central part of this ecosystem is the editor and orchestration layer — the experience where customers design and manage complex marketing workflows. From creating multi-step flows to coordinating campaigns across channels, this area sits at the heart of how customers use Klaviyo to operate their marketing. The team focuses on making powerful automation accessible and intuitive, helping customers translate complex marketing strategies into simple, scalable workflows that drive growth and retention. The Role We’re looking for a Lead Product Manager to own product development for Klaviyo’s Orchestration experience, with a particular focus on the workflows and editor surfaces used to build and manage marketing automation. Flows are one of the most critical parts of the Klaviyo platform, enabling brands to create sophisticated lifecycle marketing programs. In this role, you will shape the future of how customers design, manage, and optimize these workflows, ensuring powerful capabilities remain intuitive and easy to use. You’ll partner closely with engineering, design, data, and go-to-market teams to evolve the orchestration experience across the platform. This role will work with an engineering team of roughly 8–10 engineers and collaborate closely with another Product Manager in the broader core product area. This is a highly customer-facing role where deep empathy for marketers and a strong design sensibility are essential. The ideal candidate is comfortable translating complex systems into simple, elegant product experiences and regularly engaging directly with customers to inform product direction. What You’ll Do Own product strategy and roadmap for key orchestration experiences across flows, campaigns, and the Klaviyo editor Lead the evolution of Flows, one of Klaviyo’s most widely used automation capabilities Simplify complex marketing workflows into intuitive, scalable product experiences Partner closely with design to deliver thoughtful, high-quality UX for core product surfaces Work with engineering to build reliable, scalable systems that support sophisticated automation use cases Meet directly with customers (virtually and in-person) to gather feedback and deeply understand their workflow challenges Define and track success metrics including adoption, engagement, and gross churn Collaborate cross-functionally with data, support, sales, and marketing teams to ensure successful product adoption Who You Are 5-7+ years of product management experience, ideally working on complex SaaS or workflow-based products Experience building customer-facing product experiences with a strong UX focus Demonstrated ability to simplify complex systems or workflows into intuitive user experiences Strong product design instincts and experience partnering closely with design teams Comfortable working closely with engineering on technically complex products Experience owning core product surfaces with high usage and business impact Highly customer-centric, with experience speaking directly with customers to inform product decisions Strong communicator who can align cross-functional teams around product strategy and execution Why This Role Matters Marketing automation is the core of the Klaviyo platform, and orchestration is where that power comes to life for customers. The workflows and editor experiences you’ll help shape determine how easily brands can translate complex marketing strategies into real, automated customer journeys. By making these powerful capabilities intuitive and scalable, this role directly impacts how effectively businesses engage their customers, drive revenue, and grow with Klaviyo. The work you lead will influence some of the most widely used surfaces in the product and play a critical role in ensuring Klaviyo remains the platform marketers rely on to power sophisticated lifecycle marketing at scale. Lead Product Manager, Data Management/Governance As Klaviyo continues to expand its data platform and invest in AI-driven capabilities, the ability to manage, govern, and structure data effectively becomes increasingly important. The Data Management and Governance team plays a foundational role in ensuring that teams across the company can build reliable products, analytics, and AI systems on top of trusted data. In this role, you will help shape the systems that define how data is managed across the platform, enabling teams to move faster while maintaining the quality, reliability, and governance required to operate at scale. About the Team The Data Management and Governance team is responsible for building the foundational systems that ensure Klaviyo’s data is reliable, discoverable, and trusted across the platform. As Klaviyo continues to scale its data ecosystem and invest in AI-powered capabilities, strong governance and data management infrastructure are critical to enabling teams to build intelligent, data-driven products. This team focuses on creating the frameworks, tooling, and standards that support how data is structured, modeled, and accessed across the organization. This includes investments in areas such as data governance, metadata management, semantic layers, and data discoverability, ensuring that teams can confidently build analytics, automation, and AI-powered experiences on top of high-quality data. Sitting at the intersection of data platform, analytics infrastructure, and AI, the team partners closely with engineering, data, and product teams to design systems that scale with Klaviyo’s growing data needs. The Role We’re looking for a Lead Product Manager to drive the strategy and execution for Klaviyo’s Data Management and Governance capabilities. In this role, you will define how data is structured, governed, and made accessible across the platform to support internal teams and customer-facing products. You’ll work closely with engineering, data platform teams, and AI/ML teams to design scalable systems that support reliable data usage across the company. This includes evaluating build vs. buy decisions for key infrastructure components, developing semantic data layers, and improving the ways teams discover and interact with data. This role is ideal for a product leader with strong systems thinking who enjoys solving complex platform problems and building foundational infrastructure that unlocks innovation across the organization. What You’ll Do Define and own the product vision and roadmap for Klaviyo’s data management and governance capabilities. Partner closely with engineering and data platform teams to design scalable systems for data governance, metadata management, lineage, and data quality. Drive the development of semantic data layers and modeling frameworks that simplify how teams interact with complex data systems. Evaluate and lead build vs. buy decisions for components of the data management stack. Collaborate with AI/ML teams to ensure data systems support emerging AI-powered and agent-based capabilities. Define and track success metrics around data quality, governance adoption, discoverability, and platform reliability. Lead cross-functional initiatives that improve how data is structured, documented, and accessed across the organization. Communicate product strategy, tradeoffs, and technical decisions clearly across engineering, leadership, and cross-functional partners. Who You Are 7–10+ years of product management experience, with meaningful experience building data platforms, infrastructure products, or data tooling. Strong technical depth with the ability to work closely with engineers on system design and architecture decisions. Experience working with data governance frameworks, metadata systems, semantic layers, or analytics infrastructure. Comfortable operating in highly technical environments involving large-scale data systems or AI/ML platforms. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to define clear metrics, KPIs, and system performance indicators. Experience evaluating build vs. buy decisions for complex infrastructure products. Excellent cross-functional communicator who can align engineering, data, and product stakeholders around a shared strategy. An engineering or technical background is highly valued but not required. Developer-Centric Thinker : You deeply empathize with engineers and have experience making their workflows easier, faster, and safer. AI-Forward Mindset : You understand the transformative potential of AI in software development and can separate hype from practical value. Metrics-Oriented : You are fluent in measuring success via engineering productivity, quality, and reliability metrics (e.g., PR throughput, CFR, incident rates, DevEx indices). Influential Collaborator : You can partner with senior engineering leaders (Infra, SRE, QE, product teams) and influence without authority. Bias for Action : You thrive in fast-moving environments, balance short-term wins with long-term strategy, and bias toward automation and self-service. Success in This Role Looks Like: Klaviyo engineers feel they can deliver faster, safer, and with higher confidence than ever before. AI tools are seamlessly integrated into workflows, with widespread adoption and measurable productivity gains. Development cycle times shrink, while incidents, defect escapes, and change failure rates decrease. Developers rank Klaviyo’s internal tooling and environment as best-in-class, making it a competitive advantage in attracting and retaining talent. The Dev Infra & SRE product strategy is clear, measurable, and directly tied to Klaviyo’s ability to deliver customer value quickly and reliably. About the Team The Groups Platform team is responsible for building and operating the foundational audience systems that power Klaviyo. Segmentation is one of the most critical capabilities in the product, enabling customers to deliver personalized, high-performing marketing that drives engagement, revenue, and retention. As part of Klaviyo’s Data Infrastructure pillar, this team owns the systems behind lists and segments—ensuring they are performant, reliable, and scalable, as data volumes and use cases grow. These systems sit at the intersection of data platform, product experience, and business impact, powering how customers define, analyze, and activate their audiences. Segmentation is a key reason customers choose Klaviyo over other platforms. Research shows that customers who effectively use segments see improved ROI, lower churn, and increased expansion. At the same time, customer needs are evolving, and its the team’s mission to deliver advanced expressiveness, faster iteration, and deeper insights into performance and what actually drives results. The Role We’re looking for a Lead Product Manager to drive the strategy and execution of Klaviyo’s segmentation platform. In this role, you will define how audiences are created, evaluated, and leveraged across the product to enable precise and effective targeting at scale. You’ll work closely with cross-functional partners to build a segmentation system that is fast, reliable, and expressive. This includes improving performance and correctness of segment evaluation, expanding the flexibility of segment definitions, and enabling customers to better understand and optimize their audiences. This role is ideal for a product leader with strong systems thinking who enjoys solving complex data problems while also delivering intuitive, high-impact customer-facing capabilities. What You’ll Do Define and own the product vision and roadmap for Klaviyo’s segmentation platform. Build and operate segmentation as a first-class data product, optimized for correctness, performance, and scale. Partner with engineering to improve system performance, including segmentation latency, reliability, and SLA adherence. Evolve the expressiveness of segmentation (e.g., nested logic, advanced conditions) to meet the needs of mid-market and enterprise customers. Ensure segment definitions are consistent, explainable, and resilient as data complexity grows. Develop capabilities that help customers analyze, iterate on, and optimize high-value audiences. Introduce recommendations and insights to guide customers toward effective segmentation strategies based on best practices and their own data. Define and track key metrics, including latency (P95), success rate, freshness and correctness SLAs, and system reliability. Lead cross-functional initiatives across product, engineering, data, and go-to-market teams to improve how audiences are defined and activated. Communicate product strategy, tradeoffs, and technical decisions clearly across stakeholders. Who You Are 7–10+ years of product management experience, with meaningful experience building data platforms, infrastructure products, or technically complex systems. Strong technical depth with the ability to work closely with engineers on system design, performance, and scalability. Experience working with large-scale data systems, query systems, or real-time/near-real-time evaluation systems. Track record of building customer-facing products on top of complex backend systems. Strong analytical skills with experience defining and managing system-level metrics and SLAs. Ability to balance infrastructure investments with user-facing product improvements. Excellent cross-functional communicator who can align engineering, product, and business stakeholders. Experience in marketing technology, personalization systems, or audience platforms is a strong plus. Why This Role Matters Segmentation is at the heart of Klaviyo’s ability to deliver value to customers. It directly impacts customer outcomes—from campaign performance to retention and expansion—and underpins the company’s broader personalization strategy. In this role, you will shape the systems that enable customers to understand and act on their data with precision. By improving the performance, flexibility, and intelligence of segmentation, you will unlock more advanced use cases, drive better business outcomes for customers, and reinforce Klaviyo’s position as a leader in data-driven marketing. About the Role Klaviyo is seeking a Senior Lead Product Manager to drive our Enterprise product strategy and lead the evolution of our platform for the world’s most sophisticated, data-driven brands. This role sits at the center of one of Klaviyo’s most important growth opportunities: becoming the trusted B2C CRM for Enterprise customers. You will own multiple product areas critical to our Enterprise initiatives—including unifying customer data, powering AI-driven automation, and expanding our ecosystem integrations—while partnering closely with Product, Engineering, Design, Go-to-Market and Partner teams. This is a highly visible role that balances long-term platform strategy with near-term delivery, ensuring we meet enterprise customer expectations without compromising scalability or reliability. What You’ll Do: Set and evangelize a 2–3 year vision for Klaviyo’s Enterprise platform that delivers scalability, flexibility, and enterprise-grade governance while preserving ease of use. Translate company goals into clear OKRs and roadmaps that accelerate Enterprise adoption, retention, and ARR growth. Represent the voice of large, global customers in strategic product discussions. B2C CRM for Enterprise: Deliver capabilities that unify customer data across channels, enable real-time personalization, and support global data governance and compliance. Collaborate deeply with Engineering, Design, Sales, Solutions, Customer Success, Security, and Legal to deliver cohesive enterprise experiences. Lead roadmap reviews, facilitate strategic tradeoffs, and ensure alignment across teams. Act as a central point of coordination for enterprise commitments from RFP support and security reviews through onboarding and long-term success. Transform workflows by putting AI at the center, building smarter systems and ways of working from the ground up. What You Bring: 12+ years of experience in Product Management with significant exposure to Enterprise SaaS products. Proven success leading enterprise-grade or platform products such as CRM, marketing automation, customer data platforms, analytics, data infrastructure, or AI-driven systems. Direct experience supporting large enterprise customers (e.g., Fortune 1000), not just SMB or self-serve products. Strong intuition for what drives enterprise purchasing decisions: trust, compliance, scalability, configurability, integrations, and roadmap credibility. Hands-on experience partnering with Sales, Solutions Engineering, and Customer Success on enterprise deals. Comfortable discussing system architecture, data models, APIs, integrations, data flows, and security standards with engineering teams. Experience working on products with complex data infrastructure, distributed systems, or significant integration dependencies. Proven ability to influence and lead across Product, Engineering, GTM, Security, and Legal without direct authority. Track record of translating enterprise customer needs into clear product requirements, thoughtful tradeoffs, and scalable platform solutions. Strong communication and storytelling skills, with the ability to align diverse teams around shared priorities. You’ve already experimented with AI in work or personal projects, and you’re excited to dive in and learn fast. You’re hungry to responsibly explore new AI tools and workflows, finding ways to make your work smarter and more efficient. We use Covey as part of our hiring and / or promotional process. The team sits at a critical junction between our various integration teams and the core data platform, acting as the interface that ensures data flows cleanly, reliably, and at scale into foundational systems. As Klaviyo continues to scale, the quality, reliability, and architecture of our ingestion systems are increasingly critical to product performance and customer trust. This team will define the next generation of how data enters and powers our platform. About the Role We are seeking a highly technical Senior Product Manager to lead the strategy and execution for our Data Ingestion Platform. This is a traditional platform PM role, ideal for someone comfortable operating in deep architectural discussions and experienced with large-scale data movement and distributed systems. You will define the vision and roadmap for how large volumes of data from diverse sources are ingested, transformed, and made reliable within Klaviyo’s core systems. You’ll work closely with engineering, integration teams, and downstream stakeholders to modernize legacy pipelines and build a scalable, unified ingestion framework. This role requires strong product leadership, comfort with ambiguity, and the ability to operate at both strategic and highly technical levels. This is a hybrid role that requires 3 days/week in our Boston office. Fully remote candidates will not be considered at this time. Why This Role Matters Data is foundational to everything Klaviyo delivers. This role sits at the heart of how that data enters and powers the platform. You’ll shape the architecture that enables reliable, scalable data ingestion for years to come, driving platform health, reducing complexity, and unlocking innovation across the company. If you’re energized by building structure where it doesn’t yet exist, modernizing complex systems, and operating at the intersection of data architecture and product strategy, this is a high-impact opportunity to define a critical platform capability at scale. What You’ll Do: Define the long-term mission, charter, and ownership boundaries for a newly formed team. Develop and maintain a coherent, high-impact roadmap that balances foundational investments with active initiatives. Refine prioritization and sequencing across multiple concurrent projects to ensure focus on the highest-value work. Own the strategy and roadmap for ingesting large volumes of data from numerous sources. Think holistically about data movement, transformation, and reliability at scale. Partner with engineering to design ingestion systems that are scalable, resilient, and maintainable. Navigate tradeoffs across performance, latency, cost, and reliability. Drive improvements in observability, monitoring, and operational excellence for ingestion systems. Identify and drive architectural simplifications that improve platform health over time. What You Bring: Strong experience as a Platform Product Manager, ideally in data infrastructure or distributed systems environments. Deep understanding of data architecture and large-scale systems. Experience working with event-driven systems, high-volume ingestion pipelines, or complex data movement patterns. Comfortable engaging in highly technical discussions around pipelines, infrastructure, and system evolution. Proven ability to define structure and clarity in ambiguous, newly forming teams. Skilled at stakeholder management across multiple technical organizations. Able to think strategically while managing highly technical execution details. Attentive® is the AI marketing platform for 1:1 personalization redefining the way brands and people connect. We’re the only marketing platform that combines powerful technology with human expertise to build authentic customer relationships. By unifying SMS, RCS, email, and push notifications, our AI-powered personalization engine delivers bespoke experiences that drive performance, revenue, and loyalty through real-time behavioral insights. Recognized as the #1 provider in SMS Marketing by G2, Attentive partners with more than 8,000 customers across 70+ industries. Leading global brands like Crate and Barrel, Urban Outfitters, and Carter’s work with us to enable billions of interactions that power tens of billions in revenue for our customers. With a distributed global workforce and employee hubs in New York City, San Francisco, London, and Sydney, Attentive’s team has been consistently recognized for its performance and culture. We’re proud to be included in Deloitte’s Fast 500 (four years running!), LinkedIn’s Top Startups , Forbes’ Cloud 100 (five years running!), Inc.’s Best Workplaces , and the Human Rights Campaign Foundation's Corporate Equality Index ! About the Role We are seeking a Group Product Manager to lead Attentive’s Shopper Management pillar — the foundation that powers how our platform understands and activates customer data at scale. This team owns the unified shopper profile, identity resolution, and shopper targeting capabilities that enable brands to deliver personalized experiences across channels. Shopper Management is one of Attentive’s most critical data and growth engines, underpinning segmentation, personalization, reporting, AI-driven insights, and cross-channel orchestration. As Group Product Manager, you will define and drive the strategy for this foundational pillar while leading a team of four Product Managers. You’ll partner closely with Engineering, Data, Design, GTM, and Executive stakeholders to shape the future of customer intelligence for thousands of leading brands. What You’ll Accomplish Define and evangelize the long-term vision for shopper data, identity resolution, and targeting across the platform. Manage, mentor, and grow a team of four Product Managers, ensuring strong execution and clear ownership across domains. Drive innovation in customer data modeling, real-time updates, and scalable architecture to power personalization. Evolve deterministic and probabilistic identity capabilities to create accurate, privacy-conscious shopper profiles. Drive Cross-Functional Alignment: Partner with Engineering, Data Science, Design, Marketing, Sales, and Customer Success to align roadmap with customer and business needs. Measure Impact: Define success metrics and ensure data-driven prioritization to maximize growth and retention outcomes. Your Expertise 8+ years of product management experience, including at least 3 years of experience building and managing PM teams Proven success owning complex, platform-level or data-intensive products. Strong understanding of customer data platforms (CDPs), identity resolution, segmentation, or marketing technology ecosystems. Experience working with large-scale data systems, real-time processing, and leveraging data for personalization Exceptional stakeholder management and communication skills. You'll get competitive perks and benefits , from health & wellness to equity, to help you bring your best self to work. For US based applicants: The US base salary range for this full-time position is $241,000 - $302,000 annually + equity + benefits Our salary ranges are determined by role, level and location #LI-HB1 By applying for this position, your data will be processed as per Attentive's Privacy Policy . Attentive Company Values Default to Action - Move swiftly and with purpose Be One Unstoppable Team - Rally as each other’s champions Champion the Customer - Our success is defined by our customers' success Act Like an Owner - Take responsibility for Attentive’s success Learn more about AWAKE , Attentive’s collective of employee resource groups. If you do not meet all the requirements listed here, we still encourage you to apply! No job description is perfect, and we may also have another opportunity that closely matches your skills and experience. At Attentive, we know that our Company's strength lies in the diversity of our employees. Attentive is an Equal Opportunity Employer and we welcome applicants from all backgrounds. Our policy is to provide equal employment opportunities for all employees, applicants and covered individuals regardless of protected characteristics. We prioritize and maintain a fair, inclusive and equitable workplace free from discrimination, harassment, and retaliation. Attentive is also committed to providing reasonable accommodations for candidates with disabilities. If you need any assistance or reasonable accommodations, please let your recruiter know.

By unifying SMS, RCS, email, and push notifications, our AI-powered personalization engine delivers bespoke experiences that drive performance, revenue, and loyalty through real-time behavioral insights. Recognized as the #1 provider in SMS Marketing by G2, Attentive partners with more than 8,000 customers across 70+ industries. Leading global brands like Crate and Barrel, Urban Outfitters, and Carter’s work with us to enable billions of interactions that power tens of billions in revenue for our customers. With a distributed global workforce and employee hubs in New York City, San Francisco, London, and Sydney, Attentive’s team has been consistently recognized for its performance and culture. We’re proud to be included in Deloitte’s Fast 500 (four years running!), LinkedIn’s Top Startups , Forbes’ Cloud 100 (five years running!), Inc.’s Best Workplaces , and the Human Rights Campaign Foundation's Corporate Equality Index ! About the Role What if marketers could see, trust, and act on their customer data in real time — without needing to understand the underlying systems? At Attentive, we’re building the data experience layer that makes this possible. This role sits at the center of how raw behavioral data becomes intelligent, revenue-driving customer engagement. As the Lead PM for Data Platform Experience, you will own how customers understand, monitor, and trust the data flowing through Attentive’s platform. This role sits at the intersection of product design and platform architecture: you’ll translate complex data infrastructure into intuitive, self-service experiences that marketers and internal teams can confidently rely on. Acting as the tip of the spear for company-wide initiatives like alerting, observability, and data transparency, you’ll work closely with Engineering, Design, Support, and Customer Success to make technically sophisticated systems simple, trustworthy, and resilient — turning our data platform into a differentiated product experience. What You’ll Accomplish You’ll lead the transformation of our data platform from invisible infrastructure into a visible, differentiated product experience. Define how customers are proactively informed of data issues before they impact campaign performance. Deliver a reimagined UI experience that gives marketers transparent visibility into how their data flows through Attentive’s platform — transforming invisible infrastructure into intuitive, actionable insights. Partner with Engineering and Design leadership to build scalable customer facing tooling that decreases enterprise customer time-to-value Collaborate with Customer Success, GTM, and Support teams to operationalize data transparency, ensuring customers have confidence in the accuracy and reliability of the data powering their campaigns. Your Expertise You have 5–7 years of experience in product management, ideally working on platform, data infrastructure, or observability products at a SaaS company. You’ve built customer-facing products, not just internal systems. This is not a pure infrastructure role — success is defined by user adoption and experience quality You’re comfortable engaging deeply with technical concepts — from onsite data collection to event instrumentation and data pipelines to alerting and monitoring systems — and can translate them into clear, user-facing solutions. You’re design-minded and care deeply about product usability — you instinctively look for ways to simplify, visualize, and clarify how systems work. You’re a strategic communicator who can drive alignment across Engineering, Design, and GTM teams, balancing user experience goals with platform reliability and scalability. You’re data-driven and measure success through adoption, reliability, and user trust, not just feature delivery. You thrive in ambiguous, cross-functional environments, where success requires connecting technical depth with customer empathy. Bonus: You have experience with marketing or analytics platforms, or have previously worked on data observability, tagging, or event tracking system Wondering what your day could look like as a PM at Attentive? Take a peek at this fun day in the life of a Product Manager here! You'll get competitive perks and benefits , from health & wellness to equity, to help you bring your best self to work. For US based applicants: The US base salary range for this full-time position is $190,000 - $220,000 + equity + benefits Our salary ranges are determined by role, level and location #HB-1 By applying for this position, your data will be processed as per Attentive's Privacy Policy . Attentive® is the AI marketing platform for 1:1 personalization redefining the way brands and people connect. We’re the only marketing platform that combines powerful technology with human expertise to build authentic customer relationships. By unifying SMS, RCS, email, and push notifications, our AI-powered personalization engine delivers bespoke experiences that drive performance, revenue, and loyalty through real-time behavioral insights. Recognized as the #1 provider in SMS Marketing by G2, Attentive partners with more than 8,000 customers across 70+ industries. Leading global brands like Crate and Barrel, Urban Outfitters, and Carter’s work with us to enable billions of interactions that power tens of billions in revenue for our customers. With a distributed global workforce and employee hubs in New York City, San Francisco, London, and Sydney, Attentive’s team has been consistently recognized for its performance and culture. We’re proud to be included in Deloitte’s Fast 500 (four years running!), LinkedIn’s Top Startups , Forbes’ Cloud 100 (five years running!), Inc.’s Best Workplaces , and the Human Rights Campaign Foundation's Corporate Equality Index ! About the Role This is one of the most exciting zero-to-one bets we're making at Attentive — and we want someone who's energized by that. Our Onsite Growth team owns the highest-impact layer of the retention marketing funnel: the onsite experiences — pop-ups, banners, sign-up units — that determine whether brands can message their customers at all. The team has built a world-class list growth engine, and more recently shipped AI Grow, our machine learning-driven product that dynamically optimizes when and how signup experiences are shown. That shift — from brands manually controlling their onsite experiences to trusting Attentive to optimize them — was a significant milestone. It proved we can drive better performance and earn deeper control over the onsite channel. Now, that trust unlocks a much bigger opportunity. As a Lead PM on this team, you won't just be iterating on what exists — you'll be defining the next frontier. As brands grow comfortable letting us optimize their onsite experiences, the natural extension is to go beyond list growth and start improving their onsite performance holistically. That means enabling marketers to dynamically surface the right offer to the right user, personalizing experiences based on where someone came from and what they're likely to do next, and connecting onsite engagement directly to post-click experiences. In short: moving from "we help you grow your subscriber list" to "we help you improve your entire onsite performance." This is a rare opportunity to do foundational zero-to-one work — with real risk and real upside — while leveraging Attentive's existing distribution, customer relationships, and AI infrastructure. You’ll start with fast, scrappy MVPs with a small set of partners, before scaling across our enterprise base. If you execute well, you'll be building a major new business line with significant engineering investment behind it, and a clear path to Principal-level ownership as this evolves into a core product line. The market timing is right. Competitors are moving into onsite experiences, but most are offering basic tooling — here's a box, put what you want in it. We have the opportunity to build something fundamentally different: a performance-driven product that uses AI to optimize the full onsite experience, not just decorate it. We're looking for someone able to ship quickly with limited structure while forming a strong point of view on where the product should go. Success in this role means demonstrating measurable lift in onsite conversion and revenue, and scaling adoption across enterprise customers. What You’ll Accomplish Own a new product area end-to-end, from initial concept through scaled adoption across Attentive’s platform Identify a high-impact wedge, ship MVPs quickly, and evolve into a multi-year strategy for expanding Attentive's onsite presence beyond list growth and into full-funnel onsite optimization; initial focus areas may include post-click personalization, dynamic onsite offers, or landing page optimization. Build the next generation of AI-powered onsite decisioning — using real-time signals and machine learning to automatically make decisions brands traditionally configure manually Partner with Data Science to define the roadmap for ML-driven optimization and experimentation (A/B, MAB) that maximizes conversion across the full onsite journey Translate early customer signals and market signals into crisp, prioritized roadmaps — operating effectively with ambiguous inputs and imperfect data Develop frameworks to measure customer adoption, performance lift, and trust at each stage of the zero-to-one journey Drive adoption by partnering with GTM to bring new capabilities to market and prove customer value Your Expertise 7+ years of Product Management experience, with a track record of owning and scaling complex, high-impact products Experience setting product direction across teams or functions, and driving alignment in ambiguous environments Comfortable working across backend decisioning systems and frontend experience surfaces; you can reason about both technical architecture and the end-user experience Comfortable partnering with Data Science and ML teams — setting goals, interpreting model outputs, and translating performance into roadmap decisions Highly analytical and hypothesis-driven; able to make decisions with imperfect data and iterate quickly Proven ability to craft compelling product narratives and enable GTM success Strong bias toward action—you unblock, simplify, and drive products from concept to launch Nice to Haves Experience influencing customer behavior (not just building tools) Experience in e-commerce or martech Experience with growth, conversion, or personalization products Experience working with enterprise customers and translatin

Partnering closely with Product, Demand Generation, Sales, and Brand, you’ll ensure our story is cohesive, differentiated, and resonates with both technical and business audiences. If you are a storyteller with the technical depth to understand modern AI—and the influence to drive alignment across teams—this role will energize you. description: Attentive® is the AI marketing platform for 1:1 personalization redefining the way brands and people connect. We’re the only marketing platform that combines powerful technology with human expertise to build authentic customer relationships. By unifying SMS, RCS, email, and push notifications, our AI-powered personalization engine delivers bespoke experiences that drive performance, revenue, and loyalty through real-time behavioral insights. Recognized as the #1 provider in SMS Marketing by G2, Attentive partners with more than 8,000 customers across 70+ industries. Leading global brands like Crate and Barrel, Urban Outfitters, and Carter’s work with us to enable billions of interactions that power tens of billions in revenue for our customers. With a distributed global workforce and employee hubs in New York City, San Francisco, London, and Sydney, Attentive’s team has been consistently recognized for its performance and culture. We’re proud to be included in Deloitte’s Fast 500 (four years running!), LinkedIn’s Top Startups , Forbes’ Cloud 100 (five years running!), Inc.’s Best Workplaces , and the Human Rights Campaign Foundation's Corporate Equality Index ! About the Role Attentive’s Data Platform is responsible for collecting high-quality first-party data for our brands. As the Senior Product Manager, First-Party Data Platform, you will own our web collection layer end-to-end, which directly impacts many of Attentive’s downstream product areas to personalize every message. This role’s focus will be making the Enhanced Attentive Tag (EAT) easier to implement for any persona — from Implementation teams to technical enterprise customers to Customer Success and marketers. You will drive adoption of EAT to ensure brands are maximizing first-party data capture, while leveraging AI and intelligent validation systems to proactively improve data quality at the point of collection. Over time, you will evolve the collection layer into a more intelligent, self-optimizing, and self-service experience that increases both coverage and confidence in the data brands rely on. This role requires strong technical fluency in web data collection, paired with the ability to translate complex instrumentation systems into customer outcomes and intuitive product experiences. What You’ll Accomplish Lead the evolution of the Enhanced Attentive Tag (EAT) into a fully self-service product experience — empowering customers and internal teams to configure, monitor, and troubleshoot their tagging setup with ease. Leverage AI to proactively improve first-party data quality at the point of collection, enabling smarter validation, anomaly detection, and data completeness. Own the roadmap for Attentive’s web-based first-party data collection (client-side and server-side), defining how brands instrument and bring data into the platform. Your Expertise 4+ years of product management experience, with direct exposure to web analytics, event tracking, first-party data collection, or instrumentation platforms. Strong understanding of how data is collected from the internet — including client-side tags, server-side events, browser behavior, network requests, and event payload structure. Ability to work closely with engineers on technical systems while translating instrumentation complexity into clear customer-facing workflows and business outcomes. Experience improving implementation experiences, debugging workflows, or data quality at the point of collection in a SaaS or B2B environment. A track record of driving execution in cross-functional environments where Engineering, Support, and GTM teams depend on your product decisions. You'll get competitive perks and benefits , from health & wellness to equity, to help you bring your best self to work. Who We Are At Justworks, you’ll enjoy a welcoming and casual environment, great benefits, wellness program offerings, company retreats, and the ability to interact with and learn from leaders in the startup community. We work hard and care about our most prized asset - our people. We’re helping businesses get off the ground by enabling them to focus on running their business. We solve HR issues. We’re data-driven and never stop iterating. If you’d like to work in a supportive, entrepreneurial environment, are interested in building something meaningful and having fun while doing it, we’d love to hear from you. We're united by shared goals and shared motivations at Justworks. These are best summed up in our company values, which are reflected in our product and in our team. Our Values If this sounds like you, you’ll fit right in. About Justworks Justworks is leveling the playing field for all small businesses. We handle the nitty-gritty of payroll, benefits, compliance, and HR, so our customers have more time to learn, grow, and seek something worthwhile. Today, Justworks offices in New York, Toronto, London, Tampa and Mexico City serve more than 12,000 businesses and 200,000 workers around the globe. Our mission is to help entrepreneurs and businesses grow with confidence. This means our customers are at the center of everything we do, and working at Justworks is an opportunity to obsess over creating and delivering value that helps millions achieve their dreams. We’re united by shared goals and motivations, best summed up in our company values , which are reflected in our products and our team. About the Team / Product at Justworks The Justworks Product team advances our mission by building products that extend levels of personal care and deep expertise that our customers have never experienced before. They craft innovative solutions that guide users through the most complicated challenges of running (and working for) a small business, with ease. They deeply understand complex regulatory spaces like FinTech, Healthcare, and HRTech, and have a keen intuition for how to connect threads across our customers’ needs and our business’ risk management and commercialization strategy. Our Product Managers lead highly collaborative creative endeavors, bringing together stakeholders across engineering, design, data services, operations, and customer-facing teams to deliver tangible value. By joining us, you’ll be part of an experienced team of Product Managers who have a proven track record of building large-scale software products that impact small businesses worldwide. We’re looking for teammates who will help shape Justworks’ future and make a profound impact on our business. As a Product Manager, you’ll be working across complex spaces (Fintech, Healthcare, HR Tech - all with serious policy/regulatory environments). That will require you to have a keen intuition for customer needs, risk management, and commercialization. Care is at the center of everything we do, and you’ll need to love talking with customers and engaging with our Customer Success, Sales and Marketing teams to make sure that care is coming through in every part of our customers’ experience. You’ll be joining a diverse community of craftspeople who are passionate about learning, uphold exceptionally high standards of quality, and foster an environment of strategic thinking where product people can reach new heights. About the Role / What You’ll Work On The Lifecycle domain owns the end-to-end experience of Justworks’ customers—ensuring that every interaction, from shopping to graduation, is frictionless, straightforward, and deeply trustworthy. Within this domain, the Onboarding mission team plays a vital role in delivering a robust, quick, and accurate setup experience for our customers, ensuring they can focus on what matters most: running their business. We build and scale experiences and services that handle our increasing set of customers and their growing needs across more and more stratified workforces. We succeed when our customers discover and pick the right mix of Justworks products for their individual needs, receive best-in-class support to run their business confidently, and utilize comprehensive tools to manage their workforce. This is a high-visibility, high-ownership role at the intersection of product strategy, commercial growth, and member experience, built for someone who thinks like a GM, operates with speed, and knows how to turn ambiguous opportunities into products that deliver tangible user and business value. In this role, you will own the health of Hinge Health's digital physical therapy program portfolio, scanning for the highest-value growth opportunities, building and validating new program extensions, and establishing the operating model for how we launch and optimize programs at scale. You will work closely with commercial, clinical, design, and engineering partners across the org, and will interface regularly with executive leadership on new product bets. This role is for someone who is energized by building something new, comfortable without a defined playbook, and biased toward speed to action. What makes you a great fit 0→1 Experience Shaper: You've built net-new products end-to-end, from idea to member in market. You know how to knit together app, service, and hardware touchpoints into one coherent experience — and you think about the full journey, not just the screen.. Strategic Ownership: You operate with a CEO-like sense of ownership, taking complete accountability for your product area's performance and outcomes. You hold member outcomes, business performance, and gross margin in tension simultaneously — and make principled tradeoffs across all three Ambiguity Navigator: You thrive in entrepreneurial environments, developing high-quality hypotheses and moving work forward even when success is not yet well-defined. Bias to Action: You don't need to own a surface to move it. You know how to build the case, earn trust with horizontal teams and get prioritization decisions made. Proven track record operating in contexts where commercial or client priorities must be balanced with direct user needs.
